Capstone SolidWorks Arduino 3D Print
CaveBusters — Autonomous Mine Rescue Rover
Fully autonomous rescue rover designed for mine environments. Custom chassis, sensor integration, and obstacle-avoidance logic built from scratch.

Project Overview

The CaveBusters rover was designed to address the dangerous task of mine rescue. It autonomously navigates confined, hazardous environments using a sensor suite and Arduino-based obstacle-avoidance logic — keeping humans out of harm's way.

Build Steps

  1. 1
    Defined mission requirements: tunnel navigation, obstacle avoidance, sensor payload mounting, and modularity for easy repair.
  2. 2
    Designed full mechanical assembly in SolidWorks — chassis, drivetrain, sensor mounts, and shell — with complete toleranced drawing package.
  3. 3
    Iterated prototypes via FDM 3D printing, refining fit and material selection across multiple print cycles.
  4. 4
    Integrated ultrasonic and IR sensors for 360° obstacle detection coverage.
  5. 5
    Programmed locomotion, steering, and obstacle-avoidance in Arduino IDE with PID-based straight-line correction.
  6. 6
    Ran test cycles in simulated tunnel conditions, logging performance and refining code.
  7. 7
    Completed formal peer review of drawing package and presented final build to faculty panel.

Personal Build SolidWorks CNC / GRBL Ender 3 V2
Compact CNC Mill — Ender 3 V2 Conversion
A functional mini CNC mill engineered from a Creality Ender 3 V2. MGN12 rails, T8 leadscrews, CNC shield, and stock NEMA17 motors — minimal spend, maximum precision.

Project Overview

Converts a stock Creality Ender 3 V2 into a capable mini CNC mill. Reusing the frame, NEMA17 motors, and power supply — adding only three MGN12 rails, three T8×8 lead screws, and a CNC shield — delivers a rigid, accurate milling platform at a fraction of commercial CNC prices.

Hardware

Base Machine
Creality Ender 3 V2
Linear Motion
3× MGN12 Rails
Drive Screws
3× T8×8mm + Nuts
Controller
CNC Shield (GRBL)
Motors
Stock NEMA17
CAD
SolidWorks / Onshape

Build Steps

  1. 1
    Assessed Ender 3 V2 frame geometry; defined axis travel, spindle clearance, and work envelope in SolidWorks before any physical modifications.
  2. 2
    Designed custom MGN12 carriage adapters and Z-axis spindle mount with tight tolerance modelling to ensure squareness and minimise runout.
  3. 3
    Replaced original guide rods with MGN12 rails on all three axes for improved rigidity over stock V-slot wheels.
  4. 4
    Installed T8×8mm anti-backlash lead screw assemblies on X, Y, and Z, retaining NEMA17 motors via custom printed couplers.
  5. 5
    Flashed CNC shield with GRBL; configured steps/mm, acceleration, and feed rates via $$ calibration commands.
  6. 6
    Mounted brushless spindle to Z-axis carriage; validated spindle runout with dial indicator before first cuts.
  7. 7
    Ran test cuts on soft wood and acrylic, iterating feed rates and depth of cut until clean repeatable results achieved.
